where_servers_are_configured.description: MCP servers are configured under the `mcp` key in opencode.json, validated by Schema.optional(Schema.Record(Schema.String, ConfigMCP.Info))

ConfigMCP.OAuth.description: Nested inside Remote; set to false to disable OAuth auto-detection

interface_methods.status: () => Effect<Record<string, Status>> — returns status of all configured servers
interface_methods.clients: () => Effect<Record<string, MCPClient>> — returns raw MCP SDK client instances
interface_methods.tools: () => Effect<Record<string, Tool>> — returns all tools from all connected servers
interface_methods.prompts: () => Effect<Record<string, PromptInfo & {client}> — all prompts from MCP servers
interface_methods.resources: () => Effect<Record<string, ResourceInfo & {client}> — all resources from MCP servers
interface_methods.add: (name, ConfigMCP.Info) => Effect<{status: Record}> — dynamically add/update a server
interface_methods.connect: (name) => Effect<void, NotFoundError> — connect a specific server
interface_methods.disconnect: (name) => Effect<void, NotFoundError> — disconnect a specific server
interface_methods.authenticate: (mcpName) => Effect<Status, NotFoundError> — full OAuth flow (opens browser + waits)

variants.description: Server exists in config but enabled=false

connection_lifecycle.startup: All enabled servers are connected on init with retry({times: 4}) and 5s delay
connection_lifecycle.retry_logic: UnauthorizedError → sets 'needs_auth' status, stops retrying. needs_client_registration → stops retrying. After 4 failures → 'failed'
connection_lifecycle.watch: Each connected client registers a ToolListChangedNotificationSchema handler via watch() (line 531) — re-lists tools on change, emits EventName.MCP.ToolsChanged bus event
connection_lifecycle.close: closeClient() (line 625) closes the transport and disposes the client
connection_lifecycle.concurrency: serverLocks Map<string, Semaphore> (line 295) — prevents concurrent connect/disconnect on same server name
connection_lifecycle.tool_conversion: convertMcpTool() (line 170) wraps MCP SDK Tool into ai-sdk Tool with dynamicTool(). defs() lists tools from client and converts them

transport_types.StdioClientTransport: For 'local' type — spawns process via command array
transport_types.StreamableHTTPClientTransport: For 'remote' type with OAuth — uses McpOAuthProvider

modules.description: Persistent storage for MCP OAuth tokens, client info, code verifier, OAuth state. Stored in ~/.vibe/data/mcp-auth.json
Implements OAuthClientProvider from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk/client/auth.js — handles dynamic client registration, token storage, code verifier management
Local HTTP callback server for OAuth redirect flow. Default port 19876, path /mcp/oauth/callback

tool_resolution.flow: SessionTools.resolve() yields MCP.Service, calls mcp.tools(), wraps each MCP tool via ai-sdk tool(). Tool execution goes through EffectBridge, emits 'tool.execute.before' and 'tool.execute.after' plugin hooks with 'tool.type': 'mcp'.
tool_resolution.permission_handling: MCP tools call ctx.ask() for each execution with patterns: ['*'], always: ['*']. MCP tools are excluded from infinite-approval loops in llm.ts:162.

prompt_context.flow: SessionPrompt.build() yields MCP.Service to read MCP resources (line 808) and get MCP prompts. Resources are injected as context blocks; errors render as text blocks.
prompt_context.layer_provision: MCP.defaultLayer provided at line 1649, MCP.Service in context scope at line 1399

acp_integration.flow: ACP sessions register MCP servers via registerMcpServers() (line 932). Called during newSession, loadSession, forkSession, resumeSession. Converts ACP McpServer type to internal config via mcpConfig() helper (line 987).

desktop_ipc.capabilities: packages/desktop/src/main/ipc-capabilities.ts — mcp: true (desktop always has MCP support)

renderer_data_layer.server_sync_query: packages/app/src/context/server-sync.tsx:49-69 — loadMcpQuery() uses useQuery with key [directory, 'mcp'], calls sdk.mcp.status()

ui_components.description: Main MCP management dialog — list mode (server list with colored status dots: green=connected, red=failed, yellow=needs_auth/needs_client_registration, gray=disabled), add/edit form mode, enable/disable toggle, connect/disconnect, auth button, CRUD via sdk.client.mcp.*
Form fragment rendered inside DialogSelectMcp — Name, Type (local/remote radio), Command, URL, Timeout fields; signal-based form state
Titlebar health indicator dot — mcpIssue() evaluates MCP status: critical (failed/needs_client_registration→red), warning (needs_auth→yellow), healthy (green); factors into overall health
MCP tab in status popover dropdown — lists all servers from sync.data.mcp, shows status dot + connect/disconnect toggle + auth button; mcpConnected() count in tab label
Slash command /mcp → 'Toggle MCPs' → opens DialogSelectMcp; category MCP
EventCategory 'mcp' with icon 🔌 — events with type starting 'mcp.' auto-classified
Handles 'MCPFailed' init error with message: MCP server '{name}' failed
MCP settings tab planned but UNIMPLEMENTED — i18n keys exist in all 17 locales but no settings component
Shows 'mcp' badge label for commands with source === 'mcp'

browser_api.description: getMcpServers()/setMcpServers() — pass-through to desktop IPC, no-op in browser mode

7_mcp_desktop_ipc_and_ui.i18n: All 17 locales in packages/app/src/i18n/ contain MCP strings: command.category.mcp, mcp.status.{connected,failed,needs_auth,needs_client_registration,disabled}, dialog.mcp.*, settings.mcp.*, status.popover.tab.mcp

8_mcp_debug_events.type: McpEvent = { serverName: string, eventType: 'request'|'response'|'error', toolName?: string, timestamp: string, durationMs?: number }
8_mcp_debug_events.export: Included in DebugPacketSession.mcpEvents[], exported as mcp-events.json in debug export (debug-export.ts:99)

9_mcp_sdk.tool_wrapping: Uses ai-sdk's dynamicTool(), jsonSchema(), and asSchema() to convert MCP tools to Vercel AI SDK Tool format

capability_registry.description: Capability registry maps hooks to required capabilities. Hook dispatch guard checks trust levels and capability grants before allowing hook execution. Plugins declare capabilities; the registry enforces them.

plugin_health.mechanism: PluginHealthStore (line 51) tracks crash counts. After 3 crashes within 30 minutes, plugin is quarantined. unquarantine() resets. HealthRegistry integration at line 45.

10_plugin_and_capability_registry.mcp_relationship: The plugin system does NOT directly manage MCP servers. MCP tools are surfaced separately via MCP.Service → SessionTools.resolve(). Plugins provide auth hooks (tool.execute.before/after) that wrap MCP tool execution. The capability registry governs which hooks each plugin can register.

sidecar_process.description: Electron utilityProcess that runs the opencode backend server. Forked via utilityProcess.fork at line 32. The sidecar runs the HTTP API server on an available port.

ipc_channels.all_channels_summary: Defines ~40+ IPC channels covering: config (get/set), accounts (CRUD), auth (OAuth flows), MCP servers, custom agents, desktop state, workspace, CLI install, auto-update, doctor, sessions, logs, permissions, GitHub OAuth, and more.

ipc_contract.description: Type-safe IPC contract defining parameter types, return types, and channel strings for all ~40+ IPC handlers

mcp_entries.setMcpServers: (servers: McpServerEntry[]) => Promise<{ servers: McpServerEntry[], dropped: number }>

mcp_functions: validateAndFilterMcpServers() — validates server entries, filters out invalid ones, returns {servers, dropped}

known_issues: sidecar.ts uses Object.assign(process.env, {...}) to pass credentials — affects all subsequent code (rl-004-secrets-policy)
Desktop doctor checks (PGlite, MCP) run in sidecar, not main process — cross-process bridge needed (desktop-doctor-design-critic)

mcp_tests.summary: HTTP API integration tests: status endpoint, add/connect/disconnect flow, OAuth endpoints (unsupported errors), typed NotFoundError responses. Uses testEffect with TestInstance fixture.
OAuth HTTP API integration test with mocked handlers. Verifies oauthState preservation during startAuth.
UI component tests for DialogEditMcp form: renders in add/edit mode, Cancel button, save for remote config, save button not disabled. Uses mountDialog harness.
UI component tests for DialogSelectMcp: renders, dispose, empty state, Add MCP Server button.
IPC validation tests including validateAndFilterMcpServers() for valid, invalid, empty, null, and filter-enabled-only inputs.

no_tests_for: MCP.Service core logic (index.ts) — no direct unit tests for connect, disconnect, tool listing, watch, OAuth flow, reconnection/retry
Capability registry — no direct unit tests found
Plugin health/quarantine — no direct unit tests found
12_tests.coverage_summary: MCP has only HTTP API integration tests (end-to-end via HTTP) and UI component tests. No unit tests for the core MCP Service, auth, OAuth provider, or capability registry.

lifecycle: 1. CONFIGURATION: MCP servers defined in opencode.json under 'mcp' key → validated by ConfigMCP schema (Local | Remote discriminated union)
2. REGISTRATION: Config.Info loads MCP entries → MCP.Service.init() reads config, filters enabled servers
3. CONNECTION: Each enabled server: create transport (Stdio/HTTP/SSE) → new Client({name:'opencode', version}) → transport.start() with retry({times:4})
4. AUTH (if needed): detect OAuth metadata → McpOAuthProvider handles flow → callback server on port 19876 → tokens stored in mcp-auth.json
5. DISCOVERY: client.listTools() → convertMcpTool() wraps each as ai-sdk Tool → stored in State.tools[name]
6. WATCH: ToolListChangedNotificationSchema handler → re-lists tools on change → emits ToolsChanged bus event
7. AGENT USAGE: SessionTools.resolve() yields MCP.Service → mcp.tools() returns all tools → each MCP tool wrapped with ask() permission, EffectBridge async bridge, plugin hooks, span tracing
8. STATUS REPORTING: MCP.Service.status() → reported via HTTP API (/mcp) → surfaced to UI via IPC bridge → DialogSelectMcp shows health indicators
9. DISCONNECTION: closeClient() → transport.close() → client.close() → removes from State.clients
10. DYNAMIC ADD/REMOVE: MCP.Service.add() → stores in config → connects → MCP.Service.removeAuth() → removes from config + auth storage
13_mcp_lifecycle_summary.layer_composition: MCP.defaultLayer = layer.pipe(Layer.provide(McpAuth.layer), Layer.provide(Bus.layer), Layer.provide(Config.defaultLayer), Layer.provide(CrossSpawnSpawner.defaultLayer), Layer.provide(AppFileSystem.defaultLayer)) — wired into AppLayer in app-runtime.ts:42
